item.title: Iran vows to fight on as Trump says ceasefire is "on life support"
item.description: President Trump is expected to encourage China to pressure Iran into making a deal to end the war when he visits Beijing later this week and meets with President Xi Jinping.

item.title: Trump approves plan to fire FDA Commissioner Marty Makary
item.description: Marty Makary has served as Food and Drug Administration commissioner since March 2025.

item.title: Number of police officers killed in line of duty decreased in 2025 compared to 2024
item.description: Though the number of police officers killed in the line of duty has dropped, non-fatal assaults against them have been rising since 2021, according to new data released Monday by the FBI.

item.title: Group sues to block Trump's blue resurfacing of Reflecting Pool
item.description: A nonprofit group is suing to block the Trump administration's blue resurfacing of the Reflecting Pool in front of the Lincoln Memorial.

item.title: Trump says he has "the best plan ever" to end Iran war
item.description: President Trump spoke to reporters in the Oval Office on Monday during a maternal health event at the White House, where he said he has "the best plan ever" to end the war with Iran after saying Tehran's response to the latest peace proposal was "unacceptable." CBS News' Natalie Brand has more after Trump's remarks.

item.title: Cartel figure detained as officials recover drugs, guns and 7 tigers
item.description: Authorities said they seized unidentified narcotics, cash, 10 guns, 11 vehicles, six motorcycles — and seven tigers.

item.title: Americans evacuated from hantavirus cruise ship arrive back in U.S.
item.description: The Americans who were evacuated from the cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak are back in the U.S. They are now being monitored at the National Quarantine Unit in Nebraska. Ian Lee reports on the passengers and Dr. Jon LaPook has more on the outbreak.

item.title: Trump proposes gas tax holiday, but savings may be limited
item.description: Suspending the federal gas tax would have a modest impact on fuel prices, while also requiring congressional approval.

item.title: ChatGPT developer sued over FSU shooting: "They planned this shooting together"
item.description: The family of one of the victims in last year's deadly mass shooting at Florida State University accused ChatGPT developer OpenAI of enabling the suspect leading up to the attack.

item.title: Trump says he aims to suspend gas tax "for a period of time"
item.description: President Trump made the comments in a phone interview with CBS News chief White House correspondent Nancy Cordes.

item.title: Coast Guard seizes sailboat in Lynette Hooker's disappearance probe, sources say
item.description: The sailboat used by Brian and Lynette Hooker​ in their travels around the Bahamas — named "Soulmate" — has been seized by U.S. Coast Guard investigators.

item.title: Taiwan's goal is to "keep the status quo," deputy foreign minister says ahead of Trump-Xi summit
item.description: President Trump heads to Beijing this week for a summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping, where Taiwan is expected to be a key issue. CBS News' Anna Coren talks to Taiwan's deputy foreign minister Chen Ming-chi ahead of Trump and Xi's high-stakes talks.

item.title: What's the required minimum distribution from a $100,000 retirement account?
item.description: Have $100,000 saved in a retirement account? Here's how much you'll be expected to withdraw annually.

item.title: What we know about hantavirus cases tied to deadly cruise ship outbreak
item.description: Health officials have identified at least 10 confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus tied to an outbreak on the M/V Hondius cruise ship.

item.title: Americans from hantavirus-hit ship arrive in U.S., including 1 who tested positive
item.description: An American on the repatriation flight began showing symptoms of hantavirus and another "tested mildly PCR positive for the Andes virus," the Department of Health and Human Services says.

item.title: Dua Lipa alleges Samsung uses copyrighted image of her to sell TVs
item.description: Pop singer accuses electronics manufacturer Samsung of using a copyrighted image of her face to sell TVs.

item.title: Trump's problem is Iran war timeline keeps "getting extended," Free Press columnist says
item.description: Weeks after the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran began, the conflict appears to be at an impasse. Free Press columnist Sir Niall Ferguson joins to discuss. Paramount Skydance owns The Free Press and CBS News.

item.title: Trump says he aims to temporarily suspend federal gas tax
item.description: President Trump told CBS News' Nancy Cordes in a phone interview that he aims to suspend the federal gas tax amid surging gas prices.

item.title: Neil deGrasse Tyson talks UFO files and evidence he needs to see: "Fork up the aliens"
item.description: Astrophysicist Neil deGrasse Tyson speaks to "CBS Mornings" about the newly released documents and videos from the Pentagon on UFOs, what they reveal and evidence he would need to see to be convinced there are aliens.

item.title: Annette Bening talks "Yellowstone" spinoff "Dutton Ranch," preparing for role and her co-stars
item.description: Annette Bening talks about starring in the "Yellowstone" spinoff "Dutton Ranch," why she wanted to play her character and learning to ride a horse for the role.

item.title: Health officials discuss quarantine for Americans from hantavirus-hit cruise ship
item.description: Health officials in Nevada spoke to reporters on Monday after American passengers from the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship arrived at the state's quarantine unit. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder joins to discuss after the news conference.

item.title: Correspondents' dinner shooting suspect pleads not guilty to all charges
item.description: Cole Allen, the man accused of attacking the White House Correspondents' Dinner, pleaded not guilty to all charges on Monday. CBS News' Katrina Kaufman has the latest.

item.title: Principal who tackled gunman says he didn't initially know he was shot
item.description: Kirk Moore, the principal at Pauls Valley High School in Oklahoma, exclusively told CBS News how he acted on "just instinct" when tackling a school shooter.

item.title: Years after sex abuse scandal, a young gymnast says "it happened again"
item.description: Sean Gardner, a gymnastics coach who trained elite young girls, will be in federal court in Mississippi on Monday facing 12 felony counts of sexual exploitation of children.

item.title: Iran hangs grad student accused of spying for the CIA
item.description: Erfan Shakourzadeh, 29, was hanged after being convicted for allegedly collaborating with the CIA and Israel's Mossad intelligence service, Iran's judiciary said.

item.title: 5/10/2026: Prime Minister Netanyahu; Drawing the Lines; Gout Gout
item.description: First, Benjamin Netanyahu: The 60 Minutes Interview. Then, Gov. Landry on suspending Louisiana primaries. And, Gout Gout: The 60 Minutes Interview.

item.title: Taiwan to be key issue at Trump's summit with China's President Xi
item.description: Taiwan, one of the world's biggest diplomatic flashpoints, will be top of mind for President Xi when he meets with President Trump.

item.title: Sen. Mark Kelly says Pentagon's $1.5 trillion budget request is "outrageous"
item.description: Democratic Sen. Mark Kelly of Arizona said the Trump administration's $1.5 trillion budget request for defense spending is "outrageous."

item.title: Full transcript of 60 Minutes' interview with Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu
item.description: Read the full transcript of Major Garrett's May 10, 2026 interview with Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu here.

item.title: 5 states monitoring passengers who departed ship stricken by hantavirus
item.description: American passengers who left the MV Hondius cruise ship in April are being monitored for hantavirus in at least five states, health officials said.
